entities.name: Reduction in Services Complaint
entities.description: Tenant can file a complaint with NYS Homes & Community Renewal (HCR) to get a rent freeze due to landlord's failure to provide services.

entities.name: Failure to Make Repairs
entities.description: Landlord's failure to maintain essential services and make prompt repairs as required by law.

entities.name: Written Letter to Landlord
entities.description: Proof that tenant notified landlord of problems in writing, required for filing a reduction in services complaint with HCR or for Housing Court.
entities.source_quote: you must be able to prove that you first wrote to your landlord notifying him/her of the problem

entities.name: Tenants' Inspection Checklist
entities.description: A comprehensive list of all problems in the apartment and building to ensure nothing is left out of the written communication to the landlord.
entities.source_quote: you may want to download and print our Tenants' Inspection Checklist... and use it as a guide so that you don't leave anything out

entities.name: Certified Mail Receipt
entities.description: Proof of mailing the repair request letter to the landlord, required for HCR or Housing Court proceedings.
entities.source_quote: You must send the letter by certified mail, return receipt requested, and keep a copy of the letter, along with proof of mailing and the receipt

entities.name: Record of Previous Attempts
entities.description: Documentation of all previous attempts to get problems solved, including who was spoken to and what promises were made.
entities.source_quote: include the history of all your previous attempts to get the problems solved – who you spoke to, what promises they made, and what was not done

entities.name: Initial Repair Request Process
entities.description: The first formal step to document repair requests and create evidence for potential legal action.

steps: List all problems in apartment and common areas
Send letter by certified mail with return receipt requested
Keep copies of letter, proof of mailing, and receipt

entities.name: Rent Freeze
entities.description: HCR can order a rent freeze when landlord fails to provide required services in rent-stabilized or rent-controlled apartments.

entities.name: Repairs Ordered
entities.description: Housing Court judge can order landlord to make necessary repairs to maintain essential services.

section_b.chunk_text_preview: Getting Repairs - Met Council on Housing Skip to content The Basics of Getting RepairsThe laws and regulations of New York require that your landlord maintain essential services in your building and apartment and that they make prompt repairs. entities.name: Repair Order for Apartment Deficiencies
entities.description: Tenant's right to have specific repairs made to their apartment, including plumbing and structural issues

entities.name: Repair Order for Common Area Deficiencies
entities.description: Tenants' association right to have repairs made to common areas of the building

relief_sought: Repairs to defective entryway door lock

entities.name: Housing Code Violation Complaint
entities.description: Right to file complaints with city agencies for housing maintenance violations

entities.name: Written Repair Request Letter
entities.description: Documents the tenant's formal request for repairs and the landlord's failure to respond
entities.source_quote: If these repairs are not made by July 15, 2017, I will have no choice but to use my legal remedies to get the repairs done.

entities.name: Tenants' Association Repair Letter
entities.description: Documents collective action by tenants and specific common area deficiencies
entities.source_quote: We write to you as members of the XYZ Tenants' Association in order to get repairs to the public areas and our individual apartments.

entities.name: List of Needed Repairs
entities.description: Specific documentation of all repair needs in various apartments and common areas
entities.source_quote: We have attached to this letter a list of repairs needed in various apartments.

entities.name: File Complaint with 311
entities.description: Process for reporting housing violations to city agencies for inspection and enforcement

steps: Call 311 from any phone in New York City
Request inspection from appropriate city department

entities.name: Government Agency Complaint Process
entities.description: Procedure for filing complaints with specific city departments based on violation type

steps: Contact HPD for housing maintenance violations

entities.name: Government-Ordered Repairs
entities.description: City agencies can order landlords to make necessary repairs to correct housing code violations

entities.name: Court-Ordered Repairs
entities.description: Tenants can obtain court orders requiring landlords to complete necessary repairs
